text:
Although enhanced ORR performance has recently been achieved for the bimetallic iron–metal–nitrogen–carbon (FeMNC) catalysts, the structural factors for the improved ORR performance in acid and the active sites formed in the bimetallic catalysts are not clearly realized. To further clarify the factors and sites, the FeSnNC and FeCoNC catalysts with distinct compositional elements were prepared and characterized and their ORR performance and stabilities were examined relative to the prepared FeNC and SnNC monometallic analogues.

CO cryo-chemisorption studies show that the M-N x site density is roughly halved in FeSnNC and FeCoNC compared to that of the parent FeNC and SnNC materials. However, mass activity of the bimetallic systems is 50–100% higher than that of FeNC due to higher turnover frequency. Electron microscopy and X-ray absorption spectroscopy studies reveal that the bimetallic systems have a mixture of Fe-N x and Sn-N x (Co-N x) species without any indication of binuclear Fe-M-N x sites.

In addition, $^{57}$Fe Mössbauer spectra show that all bimetallic catalysts have a higher D$_1$/D$_2$ ratio (in relation to Fe-N$_x$ coordination sites) than their parent FeNC complex, while influencing D$_1$ site formation and hence the active catalytic species responsible for higher turnover frequencies.
